questions.question: Why should you use shallowRef instead of ref when storing a component definition for :is?
options: ref cannot hold non-primitive values like component objects
ref would attempt to deeply unwrap the component object, causing issues
shallowRef is required for performance when switching components frequently
Vue Router requires shallowRef for dynamic component rendering

questions.question: What does the 'vue:' prefix on a native HTML element's is attribute do?
options: Creates a web component that wraps a Vue component
Tells the browser to upgrade the element with a custom element class
Renders a Vue component instead of following the HTML spec's customized built-in behavior
Enables Vue-specific directives on that native element

questions.question: Why is the is='vue:' workaround only needed for in-DOM templates, not SFCs compiled by Vite?
options: Vite automatically adds the vue: prefix during compilation
SFC templates are compiled at build time, not parsed as HTML by the browser
Vite uses a virtual DOM that bypasses HTML parsing restrictions
SFCs use JSX internally which has no HTML parsing restrictions
